ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO DEFENDANTS AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL GROUP, INC., CHARTIS, INC. AND CHARTIS U.S., INC. IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE MOTION TO QUASH. SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO DEFENDANT CHARTIS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY TO ALLEGE, IF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THE SECOND PRONG OF THE ALTER EGO DOCTRINE. OVERRULED AS TO REMAINING DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ATTEMPT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR INSURANCE PRACTICES UNDER INSURANCE CODE 790.03(H); RATHER, THEY PREDICATE THE UCL CLAIM ON DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 2860. THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PROPERLY PLED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHARTIS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: James P. Wagoner of McCormick Barstow LLP (559.433.1300) for defendant and moving party; Deidre Von Rock-Ricci of Anderson & Poole (956.6413) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer of APIC and joined in by Chartis Property Casualty Company. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN PACIFIC INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: James P. Wagoner of McCormick Barstow LLP (559.433.1300) for defendant and moving party; Deidre Von Rock-Ricci of Anderson & Poole (956.6413) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained without leave to amend as to all five causes of action. The choice of law issue is an issue of law that is amenable to resolution on a demurrer. (Kearney v. Salomon Smith Barney, Inc. (2006) 39 Cal. 4th 95; Vaughn v. LJ International, Inc. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 213; Cable v. Sahara Tahoe Corporation (1979) 93 Cal. App. 3d 384 (affirming Judge Robert Weil's sustaining of a demurrer without leave to amend on the basis of a choice of law ruling)). For purposes of choice of law, the relevant issue is which state law (California or Hawaii) applies to the obligation, if any, of APIC to pay the fees and costs of plaintiffs' retained independent counsel. (Hartford Underwriters Insurance Company v. Foundation Health Services, Inc. (5th Cir. 2008) 524 F.3d 588; Northern Insurance Company of New York v. Allied Mutual Insurance Company (9th Cir.1992) 955 F.2d 1353, 1359-60). This issue is to be resolved by using California's governmental interest analysis. (Kearney). The law of California as set forth in Civil Code 2860 and the Cumis decision materially differs from the law of Hawaii as set forth in Finley v. The Home Insurance Company (HI 1998) 975 P.2d 1145. A true conflict exists because there is a material difference in the interests of the two states in their differing rules. California's rule of requiring an insurer to pay for independent counsel retained by the insured once there is a reservation of rights is based on California's interest in protecting an insured when there is a conflict of interest between the insured and the insurer. (Cumis; Northern Insurance Company). While Hawaii's contrary rule is based on Hawaii's interest in protecting an insured when there is a conflict of interest between the insured and the insurer, the rule is also based on Hawaii's interest in preserving the right of an insurer to select counsel for whom it is paying the counsel's fees. (Finley). Here, where the underlying lawsuit was venued in Hawaii and the insurance policy was issued by a Hawaiian insurer to insure a Hawaii residence of California residents on insurance forms intended to be used for Hawaiian insurance policies, the interests of Hawaii would be much more impaired if California law applied than the interests of California would be impaired if Hawaii law applied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR #11288

Jess B. Millikan, Esq., from Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, 415-352-2718, for Defendant and moving party
Deidre Von Rock-Ricci, Esq., from Anderson & Poole, 415-956-6413, for Plaintiffs and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Fireman'S Fund Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Complaint is Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action to plead whether the contract was written, oral, or implied and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs do not attempt to state a cause of action for unfair insurance practices under Insurance Code 790.03(h); rather, they predicate the UCL claim on defendant's alleged violation of Civil Code 2860. The other causes of action are properly pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
